structured_payload_candidate.title: Valve Flow Characteristics
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Three common valve flow characteristics are defined: quick opening, linear, and equal percentage.

evidence_text:
Fundamentals of Control 7.5
to both outlets. Three-wa y diverting valves are more expensive and
have more complex applications, a nd generally are not used in typ-
ical HVAC systems.
A two-way  globe valve  may be either single or double seated. A
single-seated valve (Figure 9A ) is designed for tight shutoff.
Appropriate disk materials for various pressures and media are
used. A double-seated  or balanced valve  (Figure 9B ) is designed
so that the media pressure acting ag ainst the valve disk is essentially
balanced, reducing the ac tuator force required.  It is widely used
where fluid pressure is too high to allow a single-seated valve to
close or to modulate properly. It is  not usually used where tight shut-
off is required.
A butterfly valve  consists of a heavy ri ng enclosing a disk that
rotates on an axis at or near its center and is similar to a round single-
blade damper. In principle, the disk seats against a ring machined
within the body or a resilient liner in the b ody.
judge_reason: Summary says three characteristics, but evidence only shows valve type descriptions, no flow characteristic definitions.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Valve Authority for Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: For adequate control, the valve's design pressure drop should be a reasonably large percentage of total system pressure drop (adequate valve authority).

evidence_text:
Fundamentals of Control 7.5
to both outlets. Three-wa y diverting valves are more expensive and
have more complex applications, a nd generally are not used in typ-
ical HVAC systems.
A two-way  globe valve  may be either single or double seated. A
single-seated valve (Figure 9A ) is designed for tight shutoff.
Appropriate disk materials for various pressures and media are
used. A double-seated  or balanced valve  (Figure 9B ) is designed
so that the media pressure acting ag ainst the valve disk is essentially
balanced, reducing the ac tuator force required.  It is widely used
where fluid pressure is too high to allow a single-seated valve to

judge_reason: Evidence does not mention valve authority or pressure drop percentage; it only describes valve types.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Controller Tuning: First-Order-plus-Dead-Time (Open-Loop) Method
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Formulas for calculating proportional band (PB), integral time (Ti), and derivative time (Td) based on process gain, dead time (TD), and time constant (TC) for P, PI, and PID controllers.

evidence_text:
7.20 2017 ASHRAE Handbook—Fundamentals (SI)
5. Record the proportional band as PBu and the period of oscilla-
tions as Tu.
6. Use the recorded proportional band and oscillation period to cal-
culate controller settings as follows:
Proportional only:
PB = 1.8(PBu) percent (4)
Proportional plus integral (PI):
PB = 2.22(PBu) percent (5)
Ti = 0.83 Tu minute per repeat (6)
Proportional plus integral plus derivative (PID):
PB = 1.67(PBu) percent (7)
Ti = 0.50 Tu minute per repeat (8)
Td = 0.125 Tu minute (9)
First-Order-plus-Dead-Time (Open-Loop) Method.  The open-
loop method introduces a step change  in input into the opened con-
trol loop. A graphical technique is used to estimate the process
transfer function parameters. Pr oportional and integral terms are
calculated from the estimated proc ess parameters using a series of
equations.
The value of the process variable must be recorded over time, and
the dead time and time constant must be determined from it.
judge_reason: Evidence mentions open-loop method but does not give formulas or process-based tuning parameters.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Digital Controller Sampling Interval
structured_payload_candidate.summary: A controller sampling interval of about one-tenth of the controlled-process time constant usually provides adequate control.

evidence_text:

tions as Tu.
6. Use the recorded proportional band and oscillation period to cal-
culate controller settings as follows:

Proportional plus integral plus derivative (PID):

Ti = 0.50 Tu minute per repeat (8)
Td = 0.125 Tu minute (9)
First-Order-plus-Dead-Time (Open-Loop) Method.  The open-

The value of the process variable must be recorded over time, and

judge_reason: Evidence provided is from tuning section but does not mention sampling interval or one-tenth rule.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Building Pressure for Moisture Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling climates, building pressure should be positive to keep out humid outside air. In heating climates, pressure should be neither strongly negative nor strongly positive.

evidence_text:
Fundamentals of Control 7.5
to both outlets. Three-wa y diverting valves are more expensive and
